html:
[3644]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 為政明練 - Governing with clarity and skill; indicates effective administration.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李仲吕 - A historical figure, likely a local official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 山尧祠 - A temple dedicated to the god of the mountains; significant religious site in the region.
  • 青龙寺 - A famous Buddhist temple in Chang'an; known for its cultural and religious significance.

Proper Nouns

  • 李仲吕 - A historical figure, likely a local official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 山尧祠 - A temple dedicated to the god of the mountains.
  • 青龙寺 - A famous Buddhist temple in Chang'an.

[3645]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 挈妻子南趨漢中 - To take one's wife and children south to Hanzhong.
  • 暴亡兵難 - Sudden death due to the war.
  • 揮霍不暇葬瘗 - No time to bury.
  • 庶以魂识依止饮食语言不异于常 - Hopefully her soul could rely and depend on eating, drinking, and speaking, no different from usual.
  • 悲咽告辞而去 - Sobbed and said goodbye before leaving.
  • 汝州鲁山县西六十里小山间有祠曰女灵观 - In a shrine called 'Nvling Guan' between small mountains sixty miles west of Luzhou County in Ruzhou.
  • 其像独一女子焉低鬟嚬蛾艳冶而有怨慕之色 - The image of a single woman who lowers her head, frowns, is beautiful and alluring, and has a resentful and longing expression.
  • 大中初斯地忽暴风疾雨一夕而止遂有此山 - In the early Dazhong period, this place suddenly experienced violent wind and heavy rain, which stopped in one night, and this mountain was formed.
  • 其神见形于樵苏者曰吾商于之女也 - The god appeared to a woodcutter and said, 'I am the daughter of Shang Yu.'
  • 帝命有此百里之境可告乡里立祠于前山山名女灵吾特来者也 - The emperor commanded this hundred-mile territory, can inform the villagers to build a shrine on the front mountain, which is named Nvling, and I am the one who came specially.
  • 咸通末县主簿皇甫枚因时祭与友人夏侯祯偕行祭毕与祯纵观祯独眷眷不能去乃索巵酒酧曰 - At the end of the Xiantong era, the county magistrate Huangfu Mei performed a seasonal sacrifice with his friend Xiahou Zhen, after the sacrifice, they looked around, and Zhen was particularly attached and could not leave, so he took a cup of wine and said.
  • 夏侯祯少年未有匹偶今者仰觌灵姿愿为庙中扫除之隶既舎爵乃归其夕 - Xiahou Zhen, young and without a match, now looked up and saw the divine appearance, wished to be a servant in the temple, put down the cup, and returned that night.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 黄巢之乱 - Refers to the rebellion led by Huang Chao in the late Tang Dynasty, a significant historical event.
  • 汉中 - An ancient capital of the Han Dynasty, a historically significant place.
  • 洛谷 - A place name, likely referring to a valley near Luoyang.
  • 浐水神之子 - Refers to the son of the god of the Chan River, a local deity.
  • 商于之女 - Refers to the daughter of Shang Yu, a historical or legendary figure.
  • 大中初 - Refers to the early period of the Dazhong era, a reign title of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 咸通末 - Refers to the end of the Xiantong era, a reign title of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 县主簿 - Refers to a county magistrate, a local government official.
  • 皇甫枚 - A proper noun, likely a historical figure or literary character.
  • 夏侯祯 - A proper noun, likely a historical figure or literary character.

Proper Nouns

  • 裴氏女 - Refers to a woman from the裴 (Pei) family, likely a specific historical or literary figure.
  • 夏侯祯 - A proper noun referring to a historical figure, likely a friend of the author.
  • 女灵观 - The name of the shrine.
  • 皇甫枚 - A proper noun referring to a historical figure or literary character.
  • 商于 - A place name, likely referring to a region or city.

[3646]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 惝怳不寐 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'dazed and unable to sleep,' indicating a state of confusion or distress.
  • 若为隂物所中 - Classical expression meaning 'as if struck by some unseen or supernatural entity,' suggesting a mysterious or paranormal cause.
  • 目瞪口噤 - Vivid description of being 'staring with mouth closed,' indicating a state of shock or paralysis.
  • 得非女灵乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'is it not a female spirit?' used to suggest a possible explanation.
  • 祯颔之 - Gesture meaning 'Zhen nodded in agreement,' indicating assent or acknowledgment.
  • 不胜残斚之余 - Phrase meaning 'unable to bear the remnants of cruelty,' suggesting lingering effects of past violence or suffering.
  • 慢言黩于神听 - Phrase meaning 'insulting words that offend the ears of the gods,' indicating a serious transgression.
  • 降之罚 - Phrase meaning 'punishment from above,' suggesting divine retribution.
  • 违好生之德 - Phrase meaning 'violating the virtue of cherishing life,' indicating a moral failing.
  • 专戮之辜 - Phrase meaning 'guilt of exclusive slaughter,' suggesting a grave sin.
  • 帝岂不降鉴而使神滋虐于下乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'does the emperor not descend to judge and allow the gods to inflict greater cruelty?' suggesting divine oversight and judgment.
  • 果其请 - Phrase meaning 'if it is indeed their request,' indicating a conditional scenario.
  • 一言而毙一国士 - Phrase meaning 'to kill one scholar with a single word,' suggesting the power of words to cause harm.
  • 九阍一呌 - Phrase meaning 'a single shout from the nine gates,' suggesting a call to authority or judgment.
  • 贻帏箔不修之责 - Phrase meaning 'to bring the blame of unrectified faults,' suggesting a failure to correct mistakes.
  • 言况天下多美丈夫 - Phrase meaning 'how much more so, there are many fine men in the world,' suggesting a broader context of human excellence.
  • 神其听之 - Phrase meaning 'may the gods hear this,' indicating a plea for divine attention.
  • 康豫如故 - Phrase meaning 'as healthy and joyful as before,' indicating a return to normalcy.

Proper Nouns

  • 黑水河 - Refers to the Black Water River.
  • 黑水将军祠 - Refers to the Temple of the Black Water General.
  • 太和中 - Refers to the Tahe period of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 薛用弱 - Refers to Xue Yongruo, a historical figure.
  • 仪曹郎 - Refers to a government official.
  • 守此郡 - Means 'to govern this prefecture.'
  • 魁岸丈夫 - Means 'tall and imposing man,' describing a physical appearance.
  • 须目雄杰 - Means 'beard and eyes that are heroic,' describing a commanding presence.
  • 介金附鞬 - Means 'with a golden belt and a quiver,' describing attire.
  • 延之 - Means 'to invite or summon.'
  • 遂授此任 - Means 'and was given this position,' indicating an appointment.
  • 郎中 - Refers to a government official.
  • 河上 - Means 'on the riverbank,' indicating a location.
  • 保佑斯民 - Means 'to protect and bless these people,' indicating a benevolent role.
  • 水旱灾沴 - Means 'floods and droughts, disasters,' indicating natural calamities.
  • 祷之皆应 - Means 'prayers were answered,' indicating divine response.
  • 葛谿宝劒 - Refers to the Treasure Sword of Gechi.
  • 刳神前柱 - Means 'to carve the column in front of the shrine,' indicating a specific action.
  • 匣寘之外 - Means 'placed in a box outside,' indicating a specific placement.
  • 小扉加扃鐍 - Means 'a small door with locks and bolts,' indicating a specific structure.
  • 干符戊戌嵗 - Refers to the Ganfu Wuxu year of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 大理少卿 - Refers to a government official.
  • 徐焕 - Refers to Xu Huan, a historical figure.
  • 决狱平允 - Means 'to judge cases fairly,' indicating a fair judicial approach.
  • 授弋阳郡 - Means 'appointed to govern Yiyang Prefecture,' indicating an official appointment.
  • 秋七月 - Refers to the seventh month of autumn.
  • 出京 - Means 'to leave the capital,' indicating a departure.
  • 方 - Means 'to be about to,' indicating an imminent action.

[3647]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 霖霪 - Describes heavy, continuous rain, often associated with flooding or muddy conditions.
  • 泥泞 - Refers to muddy, slippery ground, often difficult to traverse.
  • 歴崤函 - "歴" means to traverse, and "崤函" refers to the narrow mountain passes of Xiaoxin and Hangu, historically significant and treacherous routes.
  • 度东周 - "度" means to cross or traverse, and "东周" refers to the eastern part of the Zhou region, a historical area.
  • 由许蔡 - "由" means to pass through, and "许蔡" refers to the states of Xu and Cai, historical regions in China.
  • 略无霁日 - "略无" means "almost none," and "霁日" refers to clear, sunny days, indicating a prolonged period of rain.
  • 既渡长淮 - "既" means "after," and "渡" means to cross, referring to the crossing of the Yangtze River.
  • 宿于嘉鹿馆 - "宿" means to stay overnight, and "嘉鹿馆" is a specific inn or lodging.
  • 时方苦雨凄风 - "苦雨" means heavy rain, and "凄风" means cold, harsh winds, describing unpleasant weather conditions.
  • 徒御多寒色 - "徒御" refers to soldiers or travelers, and "寒色" means a cold appearance, indicating their discomfort.
  • 焕具酒祈之 - "焕" is likely a name or title, "具酒" means to prepare wine, and "祈之" means to pray or make a wish, suggesting a ritual or offering.
  • 其夕乃霁 - "其夕" means "that night," and "乃霁" means "it cleared up," indicating the end of the rain.
  • 焕由是加敬 - "由是" means "therefore," and "加敬" means to show increased respect, indicating a change in behavior.
  • 每春秋常祀必躬亲之 - "每春秋" means "every spring and autumn," "常祀" means regular rituals, and "躬亲之" means to personally perform them, indicating a commitment to ritual.
  • 明年冬十月 - "明年" means "the following year," "冬" means winter, and "十月" means the tenth month, indicating a specific time.
  • 贼党数千人来攻郡城 - "贼党" refers to bandits or rebels, and "数千人" means several thousand, indicating a large force.
  • 焕坚守城不可拔 - "坚守" means to hold firmly, and "不可拔" means "cannot be taken," indicating successful defense.
  • 乃引兵西入义阳 - "乃" means "then," "引兵" means to lead troops, and "西入义阳" means to enter Yiyang to the west, indicating a strategic move.
  • 时有无頼者以庙劒言于贼裨将 - "无頼者" refers to a rogue or disreputable person, "以庙劒言" means to speak of the temple sword, and "贼裨将" refers to a subordinate leader of the rebels, indicating a betrayal or deception.
  • 将乃率徒破柱取去 - "将" means "he," "乃" means "then," "率徒" means to lead his followers, and "破柱取去" means to break through a pillar and take it away, indicating the theft of the sword.
  • 既而晓出纵掠 - "既而" means "soon after," "晓出" means to emerge at dawn, and "纵掠" means to plunder, indicating an attack.
  • 氛雾四合 - "氛雾" means mist or fog, and "四合" means to gather from all sides, indicating a dense, confusing environment.
  • 莫知所如 - "莫知" means "no one knows," and "所如" means "where to go," indicating confusion or disorientation.
  • 忽遇一樵童 - "忽" means suddenly, "遇" means to encounter, and "樵童" means a woodcutter boy, indicating a chance meeting.
  • 遂执之令前导 - "遂" means "then," "执之" means to seize him, and "令前导" means to command him to lead the way, indicating a decision to follow the boy.
  • 既越山雾开 - "既越山" means "after crossing the mountain," and "雾开" means "the fog cleared," indicating a change in the environment.
  • 乃义营张周寨也 - "乃" means "then," "义营" means a righteous camp, "张周" is likely a name or title, and "寨" means a camp or fort, indicating a place of refuge or resistance.
  • 卒与贼遇尽杀之 - "卒" means "finally," "与贼遇" means to meet the bandits, and "尽杀之" means to kill them all, indicating a successful confrontation.
  • 张周亲擒其首解其劒复归诸庙 - "张周" is likely a name or title, "亲擒其首" means to personally capture the leader, "解其劒" means to remove the sword, and "复归诸庙" means to return it to the temple, indicating a symbolic act of restoration.
  • 至今时享不废 - "至今" means "up to this day," "时享" means seasonal offerings, and "不废" means "not discontinued," indicating the continuation of rituals.
  • 中和年 - Refers to the year of the "中和" era name, a period in Chinese history.
  • 魏博帅 - "魏博" refers to the region of Weibo, and "帅" means general or leader, indicating a military commander.
  • 罗宏信 - A proper name, likely a historical figure.
  • 本军步射小挍 - "本军" means "the main army," "步射" means "infantry archer," and "小挍" means "a minor officer," indicating a military rank.
  • 掌牧圉之事 - "掌" means "to manage," "牧圉" means "pastoral affairs," and "之事" means "the matter," indicating a specific duty.
  • 曽宿于魏州观音院门外 - "曽" means "once," "宿" means "stayed," "魏州" means the state of Wei, "观音院" means the Guanyin Temple, and "门外" means "outside the gate," indicating a specific location.
  • 其地有神祠 - "其地" means "that place," "有" means "has," and "神祠" means "a shrine to a deity," indicating a religious site.
  • 俗号白须翁 - "俗号" means "commonly called," and "白须翁" means "the old man with white beard," indicating a local name for the deity.
  • 巫有宗千者 - "巫" means "a shaman," and "宗千" is likely a name, indicating a specific individual.
  • 忽诣宏信 - "忽" means "suddenly," "诣" means "to visit," and "宏信" is the name of the general, indicating a sudden encounter.
  • 谓曰 - "谓" means "to say," and "曰" means "that," indicating direct speech.
  • 夜来神忽有语 - "夜来" means "last night," "神" means "god," and "忽有语" means "suddenly spoke," indicating a divine message.
  • 君不久为此地主 - "君" means "you," "不久" means "soon," "为" means "to be," "此地" means "this place," and "主" means "leader," indicating a prophecy of leadership.
  • 宏信怒曰 - "宏信" is the name of the general, "怒" means "angry," and "曰" means "that," indicating a reaction.
  • 欲危我 - "欲" means "to want," "危" means "to harm," and "我" means "me," indicating a threat.
  • 他日复以此言来告 - "他日" means "another day," "复" means "again," "以" means "with," "此言" means "these words," and "来告" means "to come and tell," indicating a future warning.
  • 因令密之 - "因" means "therefore," "令" means "to order," and "密之" means "to keep it secret," indicating a command to secrecy.
  • 不朞嵗 - "不朞" means "within a year," and "嵗" means "year," indicating a short period.
  • 果有军变 - "果" means "indeed," "有" means "there was," and "军变" means "a military uprising," indicating a rebellion.
  • 推宏信为帅 - "推" means "to appoint," "宏信" is the name of the general, and "为帅" means "as leader," indicating a change in leadership.

[3648]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 干宁中 - Refers to the period of Emperor Zhaozong of Tang, specifically during the reign period known as 'Gan Ning'.
  • 属夏潦峡涨湍险 - Describes a dangerous situation where the summer floods cause the rapids in the gorge to become treacherous.
  • 里俗云滟滪大如马瞿塘不可下 - A local saying indicating that if the stone (Yan Yu) is as large as a horse, it is unsafe to navigate the Qutang Gorge.
  • 行旅辍掉以之 - Travelers stop their boats and wait for the conditions to improve.
  • 学士李峣挈家自蜀沿流将之江陵 - Scholar Li Yao takes his family from Shu (Sichuan) down the river to Jingling (Jiangling).
  • 昌美以水势正恶止之 - Liu Changmei stops him because the water conditions are extremely dangerous.
  • 峣匆遽而行俄而舟覆一家溺死焉 - Li Yao hurriedly continues his journey, and soon after, his boat capsizes, resulting in the death of his entire family.
  • 唯乳妪一人隔夜为骇浪推送江岸而苏 - Only one wet nurse is swept to the shore by the waves overnight and survives.
  • 先是永安盐灶户陈小奴掉空船下瞿塘见崖下有一人裹四缝防着窄白衫青袴执铁蒺防问峣行程自云迎候乃乳妪既苏亦言于刺史云李学士至一官署上事朱门白壁寮吏叅贺又闻云此行无乳妪名遂送出水 - Previously, a salt worker named Chen Xiaonu in Yong'an saw a person wrapped in four seams of cloth, wearing a narrow white shirt and blue trousers, holding an iron spike, asking about Li Yao's journey. This person claimed to be waiting for the wet nurse. After she recovered, she told the governor that Li Yao had arrived at an official residence, where officials paid homage. It was then discovered that the wet nurse's name was not on the list, and she was safely escorted out of the water.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐干宁中 - Refers to the period of Emperor Zhaozong of Tang, specifically during the reign period known as 'Gan Ning'.
  • 防州刺史 - A local governor in the Tang dynasty.
  • 夏潦峡涨湍险 - Describes a dangerous situation where the summer floods cause the rapids in the gorge to become treacherous.
  • 滟滪大如马瞿塘不可下 - A local saying indicating that if the stone (Yan Yu) is as large as a horse, it is unsafe to navigate the Qutang Gorge.
  • 学士李峣 - A scholar named Li Yao.
  • 江陵 - Jingling, an important city in the Tang dynasty.
  • 永安盐灶户 - A salt worker in Yong'an.
  • 瞿塘 - Qutang Gorge, a famous and dangerous part of the Yangtze River.
  • 乳妪 - A wet nurse.
  • 闗承湍妻 - The wife of Guan Chengtuan, a historical figure.
  • 李冰祠 - A temple dedicated to Li Bing, a famous hydraulic engineer from the Warring States period.
  • 郑君雄 - Zheng Junxiong, a historical figure.
  • 钟离王祠 - A temple dedicated to Zhongli Wang, a legendary figure.
  • 盘古祠 - A temple dedicated to Pangu, a mythical creator deity.

Proper Nouns

  • 唐干宁中 - The period of Emperor Zhaozong of Tang.
  • 防州刺史 - A local governor.
  • 夏潦峡涨湍险 - Dangerous conditions in the gorge.
  • 滟滪大如马瞿塘不可下 - Local saying about the Yan Yu stone.
  • 学士李峣 - Scholar Li Yao.
  • 江陵 - Jingling.
  • 永安盐灶户 - Salt worker in Yong'an.
  • 瞿塘 - Qutang Gorge.
  • 乳妪 - Wet nurse.
  • 闗承湍妻 - Wife of Guan Chengtuan.
  • 李冰祠 - Temple dedicated to Li Bing.
  • 郑君雄 - Zheng Junxiong.
  • 钟离王祠 - Temple dedicated to Zhongli Wang.
  • 盘古祠 - Temple dedicated to Pangu.

[3649]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 便希回桡以成禮也 - I hope you will turn your boat back to complete the ceremony.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 狄仁杰 - A famous Tang Dynasty official known for his integrity and wisdom, often depicted in Chinese literature and folklore.
  • 大孤神 - A deity associated with a specific temple or shrine, possibly a local god.
  • 庐山 - A famous mountain in Jiangxi Province, known for its scenic beauty and cultural significance.
  • 北梦琐言 - A collection of anecdotes and stories from the Tang Dynasty, often used as a source for historical and cultural information.

Proper Nouns

  • 狄仁杰 - A famous Tang Dynasty official.
  • 葛氏妇 - A woman from the family of Ge.
  • 马希声 - A person's name.
  • 杨镳 - A person's name.
  • 相国收 - A high-ranking official.
  • 大孤神 - A deity.
  • 庐山 - A famous mountain in Jiangxi Province.
  • 北梦琐言 - A collection of anecdotes and stories from the Tang Dynasty.
  • 张璟 - A person's name.
  • 干宁 - A period in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 桂州 - A region in southern China.
  • 衡州 - A region in southern China.
  • 犬嘷滩 - A place name.
  • 江庙 - A temple by the river.
  • 刘山甫 - A person's name.
  • 挍书郎廖隲 - A person's name.

[3650]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 忘其名 - Forgetting his name, suggesting that the person's name is not known or is omitted.
  • 正直检身 - Upright and self-disciplined, describing a person's character.
  • 幕府所重 - Highly valued by the military office, indicating the person's importance in the military context.
  • 奉使湖湘 - Sent on a mission to Huaxiang, referring to a diplomatic or official trip.
  • 复命在道 - Returning with a report on the way, indicating the person was on a return journey.
  • 遇贼同行皆死 - Encountered bandits, and all those traveling with him died, describing a dangerous encounter.
  • 唯崔仓皇中忽有人引路获免 - Only Cui, in a state of panic, suddenly had someone guide him and he escaped, describing a miraculous escape.
  • 中途复患痁疾 - Later fell ill with intermittent fever, indicating a sudden illness.
  • 求药无所 - Could not find medicine, describing the difficulty in obtaining medical treatment.
  • 途次延平津庙梦为庙神赐药三丸服之惊觉顿愈 - On the way, he dreamed of a temple deity giving him three pills, which he took and woke up suddenly cured, describing a supernatural healing.
  • 彭城刘山甫自云外祖李敬彞为郎中宅在东都毓财坊土地最灵家人张行周事之有应未大水前预梦告张求饮食至其日率其遏水头并不冲圮李宅 - Complex sentence describing a story about Liu Shanfu's grandfather, Li Jingyi, and how a temple deity warned them of an impending flood, allowing them to take preventive measures.
  • 干宁中因梦金甲神自称吴安王许助开凿及觉言于賔寮因命判官刘山甫往设祭祭未终海内狞怪俱见山甫憩于僧院凭高观之风雷暴兴见一物非鱼非龙鳞黄鬛赤凡三日风雷乃霁已别开一港甚便行旅驿表以闻赐号甘棠港闽从事刘山甫乃中朝旧族也着金溪闲谈十二卷具载其事 - Complex sentence describing a dream where a golden-armored deity promised to help with construction, and the subsequent events leading to the creation of a new harbor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 崔从事 - Refers to a specific official, likely a minor official in the Tang or Song dynasty.
  • 王审知 - Refers to Wang Shien-chih, a historical figure who was a military governor and later a king of the Min kingdom.
  • 延平津庙 - Refers to a temple in Yanshou, known for its deities and miracles.
  • 甘棠港 - Refers to a harbor named after the sweet osmanthus tree, likely a significant location in the region.
  • 金溪闲谈 - Refers to a collection of anecdotes and stories written by Liu Shanfu, a scholar from the Northern Song dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 崔从事 - Cui, an official title.
  • 王审知 - Wang Shien-chih, a historical figure.
  • 延平津庙 - Yanshou Temple.
  • 甘棠港 - Gantang Harbor.
  • 金溪闲谈 - *Jinxi Xiantan* (闲谈十二卷), a collection of anecdotes and stories.

[3651]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遣道士 - Sending a Taoist priest.
  • 祷 - To pray.
  • 怳然若梦见 - Suddenly as if dreaming.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • The *太平广记* is a vast collection of Chinese stories, legends, and historical anecdotes, compiled during the Song Dynasty.
  • The *钦定四库全书* is an imperial compilation of Chinese literature, considered one of the most significant cultural projects in Chinese history.
  • The title '明宗' likely refers to Emperor Mingzong of Later Tang (Li Cunxu), a ruler of the Later Tang Dynasty (923–936).
  • Taiyuan was a key military and political center during the Tang and Later Tang dynasties.
  • Zhao Xiangzi (d. 425 BCE) was a prominent figure in the Warring States period, known for his cunning and political acumen.
  • Clay figures or idols were common in ancient Chinese folk religion and were often used in rituals or as symbols of deities.
  • The phrase '避位而立' reflects the Confucian practice of showing respect by stepping aside for someone of higher status.
  • The phrase '迭享大位' suggests a pattern of alternating periods of power or influence, possibly referring to political or familial dynamics.

  • 糜竺家當有火厄 - Means 'There will be a fire disaster at Mi Zhi's house.'
  • 萬不遺一 - Means 'not leaving a single item intact.'
  • 賴君能恻愍枯骨 - Means 'Thanks to your compassion for the dead.'
  • 故來禳卻此火 - Means 'so they came to avert this fire.'
  • 當使君財物不盡 - Means 'so that your wealth will not be entirely lost.'
  • 竺乃掘溝渠周繞其庫內 - Means 'Mi Zhi then dug ditches around his storage area.'
  • 旬日火從庫內起 - Means 'After ten days, the fire started inside the storage.'
  • 燒其珠玉十分得一 - Means 'Only one-tenth of the pearls and jade survived.'
  • 皆是陽燧得旱烁自能燒物也 - Means 'All of them were affected by the heat of the sun, which can ignite things.'
  • 火盛之時見數十青衣童子來撲火 - Means 'When the fire was at its peak, several young men in blue clothes came to put it out.'
  • 有青氣如雲覆火上即滅 - Means 'A green cloud-like vapor covered the fire and extinguished it.'
  • 家人乃收集防防數千頭養於池渠之中厭火也 - Means 'The family then collected thousands of storks and kept them in the ponds and channels to prevent fire.'
  • 竺歎曰人生財運有限不得盈溢 - Means 'Mi Zhi sighed, 'Human life and fortune are limited and cannot be excessive.'
  • 竺懼為身之患 - Means 'Mi Zhi feared it would be a disaster for himself.'
  • 時三國交兵軍用萬倍 - Means 'At that time, the three kingdoms were at war, and military expenses were tenfold.'
  • 乃輸其珍寶車服以助先主 - Means 'He then donated his treasures, vehicles, and clothing to support Liu Bei.'
  • 騣馬千匹 - Means 'A thousand horses.'
  • 及蜀破後無所有飲恨而終 - Means 'After Shu was destroyed, he had nothing left and died with regret.'
  • 出王子年拾遺記 - Means 'From Wang Zi's *Shiyi Ji* (Book of Lost Remains).'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 糜竺 - Mi Zhi, a prominent figure in the Three Kingdoms period, known for his wealth and support of Liu Bei.
  • 三國交兵 - Refers to the wars between the states of Wei, Shu, and Wu during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 先主 - Refers to Liu Bei, the founder of the Shu Han state.
  • 鄭元 - Zheng Xuan, a famous Han dynasty scholar and commentator on the *I Ching*.
  • 老奴 - A derogatory term used by Wang Bi to refer to Zheng Xuan.
  • 雜語 - Refers to a collection of miscellaneous sayings or anecdotes.
  • 陳仙 - A proper noun referring to a historical figure or text, possibly related to the Three Kingdoms period or classical literature.

[3701]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 病巫啓云 - Classical expression meaning 'the diviner reported that' or 'the diviner said that,' indicating a supernatural or mystical account.
  • 有着绢巾 - Literally means 'a ghost wearing a silk cap,' where '' refers to a ghost or spirit, and '绢巾' is a silk cap or headdress.
  • 似是故将相 - Means 'seemed to be former generals or ministers,' referring to the ghost's appearance resembling that of high-ranking officials from the past.
  • 呵叱初不顾 - Means 'initially did not heed the rebuke' or 'did not listen to the scolding,' indicating the ghost's disregard for warnings.
  • 径进入宫 - Means 'directly entered the palace,' describing the ghost's bold action.
  • 衣巾悉如其言 - Means 'dressed exactly as described,' referring to the ghost's attire matching the description given by the diviner.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鲁肃 - Refers to Lu Su (172–217), a prominent general and strategist during the late Eastern Han dynasty and the Three Kingdoms period. He was a key figure in the founding of the state of Eastern Wu.
  • 孙权 - Refers to Sun Quan (182–252), the founder of the state of Eastern Wu and the ruler who established the Wu dynasty during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 太平广记 - A vast collection of stories and anecdotes compiled during the Song dynasty, covering a wide range of topics including supernatural events, historical anecdotes, and moral tales.
  • 钦定四库全书 - The 'Four Treasuries,' an enormous collection of Chinese literature compiled during the Qing dynasty, which includes the '太平广记' among its many works.

Proper Nouns

  • 鲁肃 - A historical figure and general during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 孙权 - The founder of the state of Eastern Wu.
  • 太平广记 - A classical Chinese anthology of stories and anecdotes.
  • 钦定四库全书 - The 'Four Treasuries,' a massive compilation of Chinese literature.

[3705]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 孝武帝太元末 - Refers to the late period of Emperor Wu of Jin's Taiyuan era.
  • 妇形变爲 - Can be read as 'his wife's form changed into a ghost' or 'the ghost transformed into his wife's form.'
  • 羡因斫之见跳出抚掌大笑 - Can be read as 'Yang Xian then cut at it, and the ghost jumped out, clapping and laughing' or 'Yang Xian then saw the ghost jump out, clapping and laughing.'
  • 去羡始悟视妇成十余段 - Can be read as 'After the ghost left, Yang Xian realized what had happened and saw his wife cut into more than ten pieces' or 'After the ghost left, Yang Xian realized that his wife had been cut into more than ten pieces.'
  • 妇妊身殆六月腹内儿发已生 - Can be read as 'His wife was pregnant for almost six months, and the baby in her womb had hair' or 'His wife was pregnant for almost six months, and the baby in her womb had already grown hair.'
  • 羡惋痛而死 - Can be read as 'Yang Xian was filled with regret and pain and died' or 'Yang Xian died out of regret and pain.'
  • 亡后形见于其母刘及妻韩 - Can be read as 'After his death, his form appeared to his mother Liu and his wife Han' or 'After his death, his form appeared to his mother Liu and his wife Han.'
  • 共语就母索酒举杯与之曰好酒 - Can be read as 'They spoke together and asked his mother for wine, raising the cup and saying, 'Good wine'' or 'They spoke together and asked his mother for wine, and she raised the cup and said, 'Good wine.''
  • 语妻曰与卿三年别耳 - Can be read as 'They told their wife, 'We will be apart for three years'' or 'They told their wife, 'We will be apart for three years.''
  • 及服终妻疾曰同穴之义古之所难 - Can be read as 'When the mourning period ended, his wife fell ill and said, 'The meaning of being buried together is hard for ancient people'' or 'When the mourning period ended, his wife fell ill and said, 'The meaning of being buried together is hard for ancient people.''
  • 幸者如存岂非至愿遂不服药而殁 - Can be read as 'If we are fortunate to be together, isn't that the greatest wish? She then refused to take medicine and died' or 'If we are fortunate to be together, isn't that the greatest wish? She then refused to take medicine and died.'
  • 永嘉中黄门将张禹曽行经大泽中 - Can be read as 'During the Yongjia period, a eunuch named Zhang Yu once traveled through a large marsh.'
  • 禹遂前至防事有一婢出问之 - Zhang Yu went forward, and a maid came out and asked him.
  • 曰行次遇雨欲寄宿耳 - He said, 'I encountered rain and want to stay overnight.'
  • 婢入报之寻出呼禹前 - The maid went in to report and soon came out calling Zhang Yu forward.
  • 见一女子年三十许坐帐中有侍婢二十余人 - She saw a woman in her thirties sitting in a tent with more than twenty maids.
  • 衣服皆灿丽 - Their clothes were all splendid.
  • 问禹所欲禹曰自有飰唯须饮耳 - She asked Zhang Yu what he wanted, and he said, 'I have food, but I only need drink.'
  • 女敕取铛与之因然火作汤 - The woman ordered a pot to be brought and heated the water.
  • 虽闻沸声探之尚冷 - Although she heard the boiling sound, when she touched it, it was still cold.
  • 女曰我亡人也冢墓之间无以相供惭愧而已 - The woman said, 'I am a dead person. In the tomb, I cannot provide anything. I am ashamed and sorry.'
  • 因歔欷告禹曰我是任城县孙家女 - She then wept and told Zhang Yu, 'I am a daughter of the Sun family from Rencheng County.'
  • 父爲中山太守出适顿丘李氏 - My father was the governor of Zhongshan, and I married into the Li family of Dunqiu.
  • 有一男一女男年十一女年七歳 - I had one son and one daughter. The son was eleven, and the daughter was seven.
  • 亡后李氏幸我旧使婢承贵者 - After my death, Li favored an old servant who was now important.
  • 今我儿每被捶楚不避头面 - Now my children are often beaten, not sparing their heads and faces.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孝武帝太元末 - Refers to the late period of Emperor Wu of Jin's Taiyuan era.
  • 永嘉中 - Refers to the Yongjia period during the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 黄门将 - A position in the imperial court, often held by eunuchs.
  • 任城县 - A county in present-day Shandong Province.
  • 中山太守 - The governor of Zhongshan, an ancient Chinese prefecture.
  • 顿丘李氏 - The Li family from Dunqiu, an ancient Chinese county.
  • 同穴之义 - The custom of burying a couple together, reflecting the importance of marital fidelity in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 服终 - The end of the mourning period, typically three years.
  • 冢墓之间 - Refers to the tomb or burial place.
  • 歔欷 - Sobs or weeping.
  • 承贵者 - A servant who has become important or favored.
  • 捶楚 - Beating or flogging, often used to discipline children.

Proper Nouns

  • 太原王肇宗 - A person from Taiyuan named Wang Zhaoyong.
  • 永嘉中黄门将张禹 - A eunuch named Zhang Yu from the Yongjia period.
  • 任城县孙家女 - A daughter of the Sun family from Rencheng County.
  • 中山太守 - The governor of Zhongshan, an ancient Chinese prefecture.
  • 顿丘李氏 - The Li family from Dunqiu, an ancient Chinese county.

  • 相爲酹享 - Literal translation: 'the ghosts took turns to pour wine for the feast,' indicating a ritualistic act of sharing the offering among the spirits.
  • 曰有与君同名字者否 - Literal translation: 'the ghost asks if there is anyone with the same name as the person it is speaking to.' In Chinese culture, names can carry significant meaning and identity.
  • 便出谓闿曰君有贵相某爲惜之故亏法以相济 - Literal translation: 'I will go out and tell Kai that you have a noble appearance, and I am sorry for breaking the law to help you.' This indicates that the ghost has a special reason for intervening in Kai's life.
  • 然神道幽密不可宣泄 - Literal translation: 'but the ways of the gods are mysterious and cannot be disclosed,' emphasizing the secrecy and sanctity of spiritual matters.

  • 唯云宜勤精进不可杀生若不能都断可勿宰牛食肉之时勿啗物心 - Literal translation: 'he only said that one should strive diligently, not kill living beings, and if one cannot completely abstain, do not eat beef or meat during certain times,' providing moral guidance on conduct and diet.

  • The exchange '曰有与君同名字者否闿曰有侨人黄闿曰君可诣之我当自往' involves a question from the ghost, a response from Kai, and a further instruction from the ghost. The dialogue is clear but requires careful handling to maintain the flow.
  • The sentence '庾绍之小字道覆湘东太守与南阳宗协中表昆弟情好绸缪绍之元兴未病亡义熈中忽见形诣协' introduces Gu Shaozhi and his relationship with Zong Xie, followed by a supernatural event where Gu Shaozhi appears to Zong Xie. The structure is straightforward but contains a lot of information.
  • The sentence '言辄漫畧不甚谐对唯云宜勤精进不可杀生若不能都断可勿宰牛食肉之时勿啗物心' describes the ghost's rambling speech, which provides moral guidance. The structure is somewhat fragmented but can be understood as a series of instructions.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '戴承伯买荆州治下枇杷寺其额乃悮东空地为宅' describes Dai Chengbo buying land near a temple, but the phrase '其额乃悮东空地' is ambiguous. It likely means 'mistakenly bought the eastern vacant land'.
  • The sentence '承伯问之答曰我姓龚本居此宅君为何强夺' describes the ghost's response to Dai Chengbo's question, but the structure is somewhat elliptical. It can be expanded to 'The ghost replied, 'I am surnamed Gong and originally lived in this house. Why are you forcibly taking it away?'
  • The sentence '承伯曰戴瑾卖地不应见咎鬼曰利身妨物何预瑾乎不速去当令君知言讫而没' is a dialogue between Dai Chengbo and the ghost, with the ghost's response being somewhat elliptical. It can be expanded to 'Dai Chengbo said, 'Dai Jin sold the land, so you should not blame me.' The ghost replied, 'Benefiting oneself at the expense of others, how does it concern Dai Jin? Leave quickly, or I will make you know.' After saying this, the ghost disappeared.'
  • The long sentence '章授使到吴郡经毘陵有一人年三十余黄色单衣从授寄载笥行数日略不食所过乡里辄周旋里中即闻有呼魂者良久还船授疑之伺行后发其笥有文书数卷皆是吴郡诸人名又有针数百枚去或将一管后还得升余酒数片脯谓授曰君知我是鬼也附载相烦求得少酒相与别所以多持针者当病者以针针其神焉今所至皆此郡人丹阳别有使往今年多病君勿至病者家授从乞药答言我但能行病杀人不主药治病也元嘉末有长安僧释昙爽来游江南具说如此也' describes Zhang Shou's encounter with a ghost. The structure is complex and elliptical, requiring careful parsing. It can be broken down into a more readable format: 'Zhang Shou was sent to Wu Prefecture, passing through Pi Ling. He encountered a man in his thirties, dressed in a yellow robe, who asked to hitch a ride. They traveled for several days without eating. Along the way, they passed through villages, and Zhang Shou heard someone calling out to a soul. After a long time, they returned to the boat. Zhang Shou was suspicious and waited until the man left. He then opened the basket and found several scrolls of documents, all of which were the names of people from Wu Prefecture, as well as hundreds of needles. The man had taken one tube and later returned with a little wine and some dried meat. He said to Zhang Shou, 'Do you know I am a ghost? I am traveling with you and ask for a little wine to part ways. I brought so many needles because I can make the sick by pricking their spirits. Now I am going to this prefecture, and there is another envoy from DanYang Prefecture. This year, many people are ill, so do not go to the homes of the sick.' Zhang Shou asked for medicine, and the ghost replied, 'I can only cause illness and death; I do not know how to treat diseases.' In the late Yuanjia era, there was a monk named Shi Tan Shuang from Chang'an who came to Jiangnan and told of this story.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 肤体赤色身甚长壮 - Describes the ghost's physical appearance: 'skin is red, body is very tall and strong.' This is a literal description but carries cultural connotations of supernatural beings.
  • 文宣长子孝祖与言往反答对周悉 - 'Wenxuan's eldest son, Xiao Zu, conversed with the ghost, and their exchanges were thorough and detailed.' The phrase '周悉' emphasizes the completeness and depth of the conversation.
  • 初虽恐惧久稍安习之 - 'At first, he was fearful, but over time he grew accustomed to it.' This reflects a common narrative pattern in ghost stories where initial fear gives way to familiarity.
  • 亦转相附狎居处出入殆同家人 - 'The ghost also gradually became close and familiar, living and moving around almost like a family member.' The phrase '转相附狎' suggests a gradual process of becoming comfortable with each other.
  • 于京师转相报告往来观者门限叠迹 - 'In the capital, people began to report and visit, and the doorsteps were crowded.' This indicates the widespread attention and curiosity the ghost story generated.
  • 时南林寺有僧与灵珠寺僧舍沙门与言论亦甚款曲 - 'At the time, a monk from Nanlin Temple and a monk from Lingzhu Temple, along with a lay practitioner, conversed with the ghost, and their discussions were very intimate and detailed.' The phrase '甚款曲' emphasizes the depth and closeness of the conversation.
  • 元昔世尝为尊贵以犯众恶受报未竟果此身云 - 'The ghost was once a noble in a past life, but due to committing many sins, he has not yet completed his punishment, hence this ghost form.' This reflects the Buddhist concept of karma and reincarnation.
  • 寅年有四百部大行疾疠所应罹灾者不悟道人耳 - 'In the year of Yin, there will be 400 ghosts causing widespread disease and disaster, and those who are not enlightened will suffer.' The phrase '大行疾疠' refers to the spread of disease, and '不悟道人' refers to those who are not spiritually enlightened.
  • 犯横极众多滥福善故使我来监察之也 - 'Because of many wrongful acts, I have come to supervise and punish them.' The phrase '犯横极众多' refers to numerous wrongful acts, and '滥福善' refers to the misuse of blessings and good deeds.
  • 舍曰多知我生来何因作道人 - 'The monk asked, 'The ghost knows much. Why did I become a monk?'' This reflects a common theme in ghost stories where supernatural beings possess knowledge beyond the ordinary.
  • 答曰人中来出家因縁本誓愿也 - 'The ghost replied, 'You came from among humans and took the vows of monastic life out of your original aspirations.' The phrase '因縁本誓愿' refers to the karmic connections and original vows.
  • 问诸存亡生死所趣略皆答对具有灵验条次繁多故不曲载 - 'When asked about the fate of the living and the dead, the ghost provided detailed answers, and there were many miraculous events, but they are too numerous to record in detail.' The phrase '条次繁多' refers to the abundance of details.
  • 舍曰人道殊汝既不求食何为久留 - 'The monk said, 'The ways of humans and ghosts are different. Since you don't seek food, why do you stay so long?'' This reflects the cultural understanding of the differences between the human and ghost realms.
  • 曰此间有一女子应在收捕而奉戒精勤故难可得比日稽留因此故也 - 'The ghost replied, 'There is a woman here who should be captured, but she is devout and diligent, so it is difficult to catch her. That is why I have been lingering here.' The phrase '奉戒精勤' refers to her strict observance of Buddhist precepts.
  • 借乱主人有愧不少自此已后不甚见形复往视者但闻语耳 - 'The ghost said, 'I have caused trouble for the owner, and he is somewhat ashamed. Since then, I have not appeared much, and those who come to see me only hear my voice.' This reflects the ghost's acknowledgment of its misdeeds and the resulting consequences.
  • 时元嘉十年也至三月二十八日语文宣云暂来寄住而汝倾家营福见畏如此那得久留 - 'It was the year 433 AD, and on the 28th day of the third month, the ghost spoke to Wenxuan, saying, 'I am here temporarily. You have devoted your entire family to good deeds, so you are so fearful of me. How can I stay long?'' The phrase '倾家营福' refers to dedicating one's entire household to good deeds.
  • 孝祖云听汝寄住何故据人先亡灵筵耶 - 'Xiao Zu said, 'I allow you to stay temporarily. Why are you occupying the altar of the deceased?'' This reflects the cultural practice of honoring the deceased in Chinese tradition.
  • 答曰汝家亡者各有所属此座空设故权寄耳 - 'The ghost replied, 'The deceased in your family have their own places. This seat is empty, so I am staying temporarily.' The phrase '空设' refers to the empty seat.
  • 于是辞去 - 'And then he left.' This marks the conclusion of the ghost's visit.

  • 此夫人之偶也 - 'This is her husband.' The phrase '偶' can also mean 'partner' or 'mate.'
  • 夷光大笑而熟视之 - 'Eyi laughed heartily and looked at him carefully.' The phrase '熟视' suggests a thorough or intense gaze.
  • 李郎风仪亦足相匹 - 'The demeanor of Li Lang is also worthy of a match.' The phrase '风仪' refers to one's appearance and bearing.
  • 阿妇夫容貌岂得动人 - 'How can the appearance of my husband be moving?' The phrase '岂得' is a rhetorical question, implying that it is unlikely.
  • 合座喧笑俱起就寝 - 'Everyone laughed and got up to go to bed.' The phrase '合座' means 'everyone present.'
  • 临晓请去尚未天明 - 'At dawn, she asked to leave, but it was not yet morning.' The phrase '临晓' means 'at dawn.'
  • 妾本浣纱之女吴王之姬 - 'I am originally a washerwoman, a concubine of King Wu.' The phrase '浣纱' means 'washing silk,' and '姬' means 'concubine.'
  • 君固知之矣 - 'You must know this already.' The phrase '固' implies certainty.
  • 为越所迁妾落他人之手 - 'I was taken by the Yue people and fell into the hands of others.' The phrase '为...所' indicates being acted upon.
  • 吴王殁后复居故国 - 'After King Wu died, I returned to my homeland.' The phrase '殁' means 'to die.'
  • 今吴王已耋不任妾等 - 'Now King Wu is old and can no longer handle us.' The phrase '耋' means 'old and feeble.'
  • 夷光是越王之女越皆贡吴王者 - 'Eyi is the daughter of the King of Yue, and all of Yue offered her to King Wu.' The phrase '贡' means 'to offer as tribute.'
  • 妾与夷光相爱坐则同席出则同车 - 'I and Eyi love each other; we sit together on the same mat and travel together in the same carriage.' The phrase '同席' and '同车' emphasize close companionship.
  • 惘然 - 'Dispirited' or 'dejected.'
  • 导与士炯深感恨 - 'Guide and Sir Jiong deeply felt regret.' The phrase '深感恨' means 'deeply felt regret.'
  • 后会无期 - 'We may never meet again.' The phrase '无期' means 'no fixed time.'
  • 西施以寳钿一只留与导 - 'Xi Shi left a piece of jewelry with the Guide.' The phrase '寳钿' means 'jewelry' or 'ornament.'
  • 夷光折裙珠一双亦赠士炯 - 'Eyi broke off a pair of pearls from her skirt and gave them to Sir Jiong.' The phrase '折裙珠' means 'pearls from her skirt.'
  • 言讫惘然 - 'After speaking, they were dispirited.' The phrase '言讫' means 'after speaking.'
  • 音犹在耳顷刻无见 - 'The sound was still in their ears, but in an instant, they were gone.' The phrase '顷刻' means 'in an instant.'
  • 时梁武帝天监十一年七月也 - 'It was the seventh month of the eleventh year of Emperor Wu of Liang's Tianjian era.' The phrase '天监' is the era name of Emperor Wu of Liang.

  • 寳钿 - Jewelry or ornaments, often made of precious materials.
  • 裙珠 - Pearls from a skirt, often used as jewelry or ornaments.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 张女郎庙 - A temple dedicated to a female deity or spirit known as 'Lady Zhang.' Such temples were common in ancient China, often associated with local deities, spirits, or historical figures.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sequence '人世所闻警嗟赏良久愿请琴写之' must be read as a single phrase, indicating that what is heard in the world is longly lamented and praised, and the wish is to request a lute to write it down.
  • The clause '此是秦穆公周灵王太子神仙所制不可传于人间' states that this is made by the immortal Qin Mu Gong and the Crown Prince of King Ling of Zhou, and it cannot be passed down to the human world.
  • The clause '警粗记数弄不复敢访及' indicates that Jing roughly remembered a few tunes and dared not inquire further.
  • The phrase '酒酣大女郎歌曰' introduces the elder sister's song, which follows.
  • The long sequence '人神相合兮后会难邂逅相遇兮暂为欢星汉移兮夜将阑心未极兮且盘桓' must be read as a series of linked phrases, each expressing a different sentiment.
  • The long sequence '洞箫响兮风生流清夜阑兮管弦遒长相思兮衡山曲心断绝兮秦陇头' must be read as a series of linked phrases, each expressing a different sentiment.
  • The long sequence '又题曰陇上云车不复居湘川斑竹泪沾余谁念衡山烟雾里空看雁足不传书' must be read as a series of linked phrases, each expressing a different sentiment.
  • The long sequence '警歌曰义熙曾历许多年张硕凡得几时怜何意今人不及昔暂来相见更无缘' must be read as a series of linked phrases, each expressing a different sentiment.
  • The long sequence '二女郎相顾流涕警亦下泪小女郎谓警曰兰香姨智琼姊亦尝怀此恨矣' must be read as a series of linked phrases, each expressing a different sentiment.

  • 周灵王太子 - Crown Prince of King Ling of Zhou.
  • 义熙 - A reign title of Emperor Xiaowu of Liu Song.
  • 张硕 - A historical figure or a literary character.
  • 兰香 - A name.
  • 智琼 - A name.
